alternatives.name: Cold-drawn precision T-section (ZOLLERN position)
alternatives.tradeoffs: IT8 tolerance (+-0.02 mm); in-line IH to 64 HRC; <=0.2 mm/m straightness; IATF 16949 with EN 10204 3.1 per heat. Weakness: Higher unit cost; 6m max bar length (more splice joints); 6-12 week die lead for new sizes. ~40% share.

alternatives.name: Hot-rolled + post-machined T-section (ArcelorMittal, voestalpine)
alternatives.tradeoffs: +-0.5 mm tolerance without machining; no integrated surface hardening; 12m bar length (fewer joints). Requires secondary machining cost to tighten tolerance; dominant for standard/freight applications. ~45% share.

alternatives.name: Cold-formed hollow steel section (MONTEFERRO, Zhangjiagang Mofeel)
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~20% material/weight saving; lower cross-section inertia limits speed to <=1.6 m/s; non-standard guide shoe compatibility; cannot support safety gear clamping; mainly low/mid-rise. ~10% share.

alternatives.name: Stainless / aluminium special rail
alternatives.tradeoffs: Corrosion advantage; lower strength limits load rating; aluminium not hardenable by IH; premium pricing; niche residential/light-duty only. ~4% share.

alternatives.name: Machined from solid bar (legacy / repair)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 60-80% material waste; highest per-unit cost; no near-net geometry; long lead time; declining share; used only for legacy repair and specialty one-offs. ~5% share.
